questions.question: 1. The water content of a soil sample cannot be determined by ____ method.

explanations.a: Incorrect. This method can be used as is demonstrated by this very experiment.
explanations.b: Incorrect. This method is very similar to the oven drying method. Instead of using an oven to dry the soil, the sample is mixed with a methylated spirit and heated with constant stirring. Clearly, this method can be used for water content determination.
explanations.c: Incorrect. This method can be used for water content determination. To know more about this method, click here.
explanations.d: Correct. The pipette method is the standard sedimentation method used in laboratory for wet mechanical or sedimentation analysis and cannot be used for water content determination.

questions.question: 2. In the oven drying method, the soil sample is kept for about ____ hours in the oven.

explanations.a: Incorrect. Some soil types may take longer and hence 1 hour is not sufficient in general.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Some soil types may take longer and hence 2 hours is not sufficient in general.
explanations.c: Correct. For complete drying, sandy soil takes about 4 hours and fat clays take about 14 to 16 hours. To assure complete drying of the sample for any type of soil, it is kept for about 24 hours.
explanations.d: Incorrect. 48 hours is an excessive amount of time and the soil can be dried in much lesser time for any soil type.

questions.question: 3. The values of water content for natural granular soils vary from less than 0.1% for air dry sands to more than 40% for saturated sands.

explanations.a: Correct. Water content depends on the relative density of soil.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Water content depends on the relative density of soil.

questions.question: 4. Which method is considered the standard laboratory method for determining the water content of soil?

explanations.a: Correct. The oven drying method is the standard laboratory method for water content determination.
explanations.b: Incorrect. The pipette method is used for particle size analysis.
explanations.c: Incorrect. The hydrometer method is used for fine-grained soil analysis.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Sieve analysis is used for grain size distribution.

questions.question: 5. In the oven drying method, soil samples are generally dried for about:

explanations.a: Incorrect. One hour is generally insufficient.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Two hours may not completely dry the sample.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Twelve hours may not ensure constant mass.
explanations.d: Correct. Soil samples are generally dried for about 24 hours.

questions.question: 6. Water content is defined as the ratio of:

answers.a: Mass of water to mass of dry soil solids
answers.b: Mass of water to total mass of soil
answers.c: Volume of water to volume of voids
answers.d: Mass of solids to mass of water

explanations.a: Correct. Water content is the ratio of mass of water to mass of dry soil solids.
explanations.b: Incorrect. This is not the standard definition.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Water content is not defined using void volume.

questions.question: 7. Why is water content determination important in geotechnical engineering?

answers.c: For evaluating engineering properties of soil

explanations.a: Incorrect. Soil colour alone is not the purpose.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Mineral identification requires other tests.
explanations.c: Correct. Water content significantly affects engineering behaviour.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Particle size requires separate tests.
